codingSystem.description_variable: A coding system is a standardized system for encoding medical information, such as diagnoses, treatments, medications, and procedures. These systems serve to uniformly record, document and bill medical services as well as to support clinical studies and health research
conformsTo.description_resource: Established standard to which the described data conforms to
contact.description: Person or unit that can help with accessing, troubleshooting, or otherwise resolve issues related to the platform
contact.description_resource: Contact for the data source/data set
containedBy.description_variablegroup: Data source/data set, the variable group belongs to

contributingUnit.description_resource: Organizational unit(s) contributing to the data
created.description_resource: Date the data was created; this could refer to a time frame in the past, a date range, or a single date for a finalized component, e.g., the completed dataset with all the data
creator.description_resource: Person who compiled the data; creator of the dataset

description.description_resource: Brief description of the data, its components, and the context of how the data was created

doi.description: A DOI (Digital Object Identifier) is a unique, stable identifier assigned to digital objects such as scientific articles, research data, or data collections. It enables a permanent and reliable link to the object, regardless of changes to its web address or storage location
endpointDescription.description_accesspl: Description of the endpoint, e.g. documentation of the API
endpointURL.description_accessplatform: A URL that allows access to the endpoint
externalAssociate.description_activity: External institution(s) involved in the project, such as research cooperation partners
externalPartner.description_resource: External organizations or institutions involved in the creation and/or research of the data
fullName.description_person: Full name of the person. Is also used if the name scheme (first name and surname) does not apply to the name
funderOrCommissioner.description_activit: Name of the funding bodies (e.g., acquired research funds) or commissioning entities (e.g., legal mandate).
geprisId.description_organization: Identifier from GEPRIS, a database of the German Research Foundation (DFG), which shows scientific projects, researchers and their institutions
gndId.description_organization: Identifier from GND, the Common Authority File of the German National Library
hadPrimarySource.description: Original data storage (primary system) from which the information originates
hasLegalBasis.description_resource: Laws and regulations that are the basis on which data is collected
hasPersonalData.description_resource: Indicates whether the data includes personal data
hasPurpose.description_resource: Description of the purpose of the data processing, in particular for personal data
icd10code.description_resource: ICD-10 codes (International Classification of Diseases, 10th Revision) are standardized codes used to classify diagnoses and health conditions
identifier.description: All extracted metadata objects are provided with an identifier
identifier.singular: Identifier of the extracted metadata object
identifierInPrimarySource.description: Used to identify the metadata object in the original data storage (primary system)
instrumentToolOrApparatus.description_re: Tools used for recording, collecting, analyzing and researching the data
involvedUnit.description_activity: Organizational unit(s) involved in the project/endeavor
isPartOf.description_resource: This data forms a subset of the superordinate data source/data set
isPartOfActivity.description_activity: This activity is part of a superordinate project, e.g. a sub-project
isbnIssn.description_bibliographicresour: International Standard Book Number (ISBN) or International Standard Serial Number (ISSN) for journals
isniId.description: International Standard Name Identifier, an ISO-certified identifier for persons and institutions
journal.description_bibliographicresourc: The journal in which the scientific article was published
label.description_variable: Variables are defined for the data-based evaluation of investigations (e.g. studies). A variable is characterized by its data type (e.g. integer, string, date) and value range. The variable can be either quantitative or qualitative, i.e. the value range can assume numerical or categorical values
license.description: License that regulates the conditions of data usage
loincId.description_resource: Unique identifier for a specific medical test, measurement or observation in the LOINC system (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes), which is used worldwide for the standardized identification of health data
maxTypicalAge.description_resource: Maximum age of the population in years within the data collection period
mediaType.description_distribution: Media type of the data, defined according to [IANA media types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/media-types/)
memberOf.description_person: Organizational unit to which the person belongs
meshId.description_resource: Medical Subject Headings (MeSH) Thesaurus is a controlled and hierarchically organized vocabulary created by the National Library of Medicine
method.description_resource: Method used in the collection, analysis, observation or processing of data
methodDescription.description_resource: Description of the method used in the collection, analysis, observation or processing of the data
minTypicalAge.description_resource: Minimum age of the population in years within the data collection period
modified.description: Date on which the data was last changed
orcidId.description_person: ORCID is a unique, persistent identifier for researchers to reliably assign scientific work and contributions to a person from the Open Researcher and Contributor Identification Initiative (ORCID)
pages.description_bibliographicresource: Pages of the article in the journal
populationCoverage.description_resource: Indication of which part or group of the population is the subject of the data collection, e.g. infants aged 1 to 3 years or patients with type 2 diabetes
provenance.description_resource: Description of how the data was generated, where it came from, how it was collected; which data sources were used to compile the data
publicationYear.description_bibliographi: Year, when the publication was issued
publisher.description_resource: Responsible party for making available/providing the data
qualityInformation.description_resource: Information on the accuracy, reliability and validity of the data
repositoryURL.description_bibliographicr: Handle of the publication in the repository in which the publication is stored. A handle is a unique identification number or reference identifier used to identify and access a digital object in a repository
resourceCreationMethod.description_resou: Method with which the data is generated
resourceTypeGeneral.description_resource: Type(s) of data, e.g. data set, image or software code
resourceTypeSpecific.description_resourc: More detailed type of data. For example, health survey data, epidemiological data or register data
responsibleUnit.description_activity: Organizational unit(s) responsible for the project/endeavor
rights.description_resource: Information on the terms of use of the data
rorId.description_organization: Research Organization Registry is a community-managed database designed to provide a permanent identifier for every research organization in the world
section.description_bibliographicresourc: Chapter to which the book section belongs
shortName.description: Short name, abbreviated title or acronym
sizeOfDataBasis.description_resource: Size of the basis of the data, e.g. for studies: the size of the sample
stableTargetId.description: Each merged metadata object has a stable identifier. Merged metadata objects consist of individual, extracted metadata objects
stateOfDataProcessing.description_resour: Processing status of the data, e.g. raw or aggregated
succeeds.description_activity: Another project/endeavor that preceded the one described here

unitOf.description_organizationalunit: Institute to which the organizational unit belongs

valueSet.description_variable: Total of all possible values that a variable can assume, i.e. different characteristic values or values that are permissible for this variable
version.description_primarysource: Version of the original data storage (primary source)
viafId.description_organization: Virtual International Authority File is a joint project of several national libraries and library networks

wikidataId.description_organization: Wikidata is a free, shared, multilingual and secondary database for the collection of structured data to support Wikipedia and is also used as a reference database for the identification of things (in the sense of Linked Data)
